requirements.description: Volunteer labour for bioswale groundworks and native planting
Topsoil, gravel substrate, and native marginal planting materials
Volunteer time for clearance day — bagging, loading, and site tidying
Surveyor visits and structural load assessments
Volunteer cycling auditors for school route surveys

inquiries.body: Has the Drainage Action Group obtained formal consent from Eastshire Council's highways department before proceeding with groundworks at the Elm Street site? This is adopted public highway — uncoordinated excavation could create a significant liability for participants and the council alike.
inquiries.response: We have been in correspondence with the council highways team since January. A formal consent-to-excavate application was submitted in February (reference HWY-CONSENT-2026-031) and we are awaiting sign-off before breaking ground. We will not proceed without it — the bioswale design itself was developed in consultation with the highways drainage engineer.

inquiries.body: What arrangement has been made with the landowner of the Pine Avenue site? Clearing the land without a stewardship agreement risks the site simply filling again within a season. Has the Land Stewards Collective made contact with the landowner, and is there a written agreement in place or in progress?
